operating_ranges.evidence: maximum pressure at the pump outlet does not exceed 0.5 bar gauge (1.5 bar absolute, 1.5 x 10⁵ Pa) at full pump throughput
if you operate the pump with an inlet pressure greater than 10 mbar for extended periods

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: The mist filter will trap the oil exhausted from the pump
Use a catchpot to prevent the drainage of contaminated condensate back into the pump

fault_conditions.name: dangerous_gas_discharge
fault_conditions.description: discharge of dangerous gases and vapours to surrounding atmosphere
fault_conditions.evidence: Connect the pump outlet to a suitable treatment plant to prevent the discharge of dangerous gases and vapours to the surrounding atmosphere

fault_conditions.name: excessive_outlet_pressure
fault_conditions.description: outlet pressure exceeds maximum allowable limit
fault_conditions.evidence: maximum pressure at the pump outlet does not exceed 0.5 bar gauge

maintenance_actions.name: oil_mist_filter_installation
maintenance_actions.interval: when operating with gas-ballast open or inlet pressure > 10 mbar
maintenance_actions.evidence: we recommend that you fit an oil mist filter to the pump outlet if you use the pump with the gas-ballast control open

maintenance_actions.name: catchpot_installation
maintenance_actions.interval: for condensable vapours or dusty applications
maintenance_actions.evidence: Use a suitable catchpot if you pump condensable vapours or if you use the pump for very dusty applications

assumptions.statement: Exhaust system includes vacuum pump and associated treatment equipment
assumptions.reason: Manual excerpts discuss pump outlet connections, treatment plants, and vacuum operation which are typical exhaust system components

assumptions.statement: System operates with rotary vacuum pumps
assumptions.reason: References to E2M2, E1/E2M5 and 8, E2M12 Rotary Vacuum Pumps and oil mist considerations
